title: Autonomous LLM Trading Lab — Pre-Registration
author: Aadi Salhotra
principles: Append-only after the OSF deposit date; all later changes recorded in amendments[].
PRE_REGISTRATION.md / this file are authoritative; research_metrics.py is its executable form. On disagreement, the registration wins.
Inference uses live-phase data only. Paper-trading is pilot data (pipeline validation, definition-locking, power estimation).

experiment.identical_input_guarantee: all models share one data_inputs_hash per tick (verified)

research_questions.title: Decision convergence under identical information sets

hypothesis.null: Given identical inputs, pairwise decision agreement equals the chance level implied by each model's own action base rates.
hypothesis.alternative: Pairwise decision agreement exceeds chance — models converge.

research_questions.operationalization: Join records on data_inputs_hash (identical across models within a tick). Use each model's raw_decisions (pre-risk-filter). For every model pair on every tick, over tickers both ruled on (>=3 shared): action concordance (3-way BUY/SELL/HOLD exact-match rate) and weight correlation (Pearson r of target_weight vectors).
research_questions.metric_definition: Headline = mean pairwise action concordance across all (pair, tick) observations. Chance = shuffled-permutation null: independently permute each model's action vector within a tick (preserving its BUY/SELL/HOLD marginals), recompute grand-mean concordance, repeat to build the null. Excess = observed minus null mean.
research_questions.prediction: Observed concordance exceeds the shuffled-null mean by >=0.10, one-sided permutation p<0.05 surviving FDR. Strongest in trending regimes, weakest in vol-spike.
research_questions.decision_rule: Resolved-converge if live-window permutation p<0.05 after Benjamini-Hochberg AND BCa 90% CI for excess concordance excludes 0. Resolved-null if CI includes 0. Interpreted against the RQ6 non-determinism floor (convergence ceiling = 1 - within-model flip rate).
research_questions.data_window_required: >=200 pairwise (pair,tick) observations with >=3 shared tickers, live data; >=50 per regime for stratified estimates.

research_questions.title: Disposition effect in sequential trading

hypothesis.null: Proportion of gains realized equals proportion of losses realized (PGR - PLR = 0).

research_questions.operationalization: Odean (1998). Replay executed trades with average-cost accounting. On each record with >=1 executed SELL: each sell is a realized gain (fill>avg cost) or loss (fill<avg cost); paper gains/losses are still-held positions classified by unrealized_pl_pct from that record's portfolio_after. Transaction-level (partial trims count).
research_questions.metric_definition: PGR = realized gains / (realized gains + paper gains). PLR = realized losses / (realized losses + paper losses). Disposition difference = PGR - PLR (headline). Disposition ratio = PGR / PLR.
research_questions.prediction: Weaker disposition than retail humans (whose PGR-PLR ~ +0.05 to +0.10). Two-sided; pilot suggests a possible reverse disposition (cutting losers faster).
research_questions.decision_rule: Per model and pooled: Resolved when BCa 90% CI for PGR-PLR excludes 0 (live data); sign gives direction. Pooled test enters the FDR family.
research_questions.data_window_required: >=30 sale records per model (>=100 pooled), live data.

research_questions.title: Confidence calibration on closed trades

hypothesis.null: P(profitable | confidence=k) is flat in k; ECE no better than a non-informative baseline.
hypothesis.alternative: P(profitable | confidence=k) increases in k (confidence is informative).

research_questions.operationalization: Closed trades only. A position is closed ONLY on full exit (residual shares < 0.01); partial trims never close it; re-entry after exit is an independent trade. Outcome = realized P&L > 0 over the full life. Entry confidence = share-weighted average of buy-leg confidences, rounded to nearest integer 1-10.
research_questions.metric_definition: Calibration curve P(profitable | confidence=k) for k=1..10. Brier = mean((p_hat - outcome)^2), p_hat = confidence/10. ECE = sum_k (n_k/N)*|acc_k - k/10|, bins = integer confidence. Headline statistic = point-biserial correlation between entry confidence and profitable outcome.
research_questions.prediction: Directionally informative but overconfident: confidence/outcome correlation in ~[+0.05, +0.20], ECE materially above 0.
research_questions.decision_rule: Per model with >=20 closed trades (locked minimum): Resolved-calibrated if BCa 90% CI for confidence/outcome correlation excludes 0 and is positive (live data). <20 closed trades stays Open. Pooled correlation enters the FDR family.
research_questions.data_window_required: >=20 closed (full-exit) trades per model, live data.

research_questions.title: Systematic style-factor tilts

hypothesis.null: Daily excess returns load zero on each style factor (pure-alpha / factor-neutral).
hypothesis.alternative: One or more factor loadings are non-zero (persistent size/value/quality/investment/momentum tilt).

research_questions.operationalization: OLS of each model's daily excess return (portfolio return - RF) on Fama-French 5 factors (Mkt-RF, SMB, HML, RMW, CMA) plus Momentum (MOM), with an intercept (alpha). Daily factors from the Ken French Data Library. EOD values deduplicated to one per trading day.
research_questions.metric_definition: Per model: annualized alpha and t-stat; six factor betas with t-stats; R-squared. p-values via normal approximation (no scipy), flagged approximate at small n.
research_questions.prediction: Tilt toward large-cap growth/quality (negative SMB, negative HML, positive RMW) with positive momentum loading; alphas indistinguishable from zero net of factors.
research_questions.decision_rule: Per factor per model: Resolved-tilt if BCa 90% CI for the beta excludes 0 over the live window. Alpha != 0 (smallest p across models) enters the FDR family. Cross-regime loadings require the backtest harness.
research_questions.data_window_required: >=60 aligned trading days per model, live data; cross-regime requires the backtest harness (BACKTEST_HARNESS_SCOPE.md).

research_questions.title: Behavioral response to portfolio drawdowns

hypothesis.null: Concentration, turnover, position size, and cash are unchanged between drawdown and normal days.
hypothesis.alternative: Behavior shifts measurably when a model is in drawdown.

research_questions.operationalization: A model is in drawdown on an EOD when portfolio value is >=10% below its trailing 60-day peak (EOD value). Distinct from the SPY drawdown regime (5% off a 60-day peak) used for stratification. For each EOD compute four behavioral metrics from that day's last portfolio_after snapshot and trade activity; compare drawdown vs normal days.
research_questions.metric_definition: Drawdown-minus-normal difference in: (1) HHI concentration = sum w_i^2; (2) turnover = sum |executed notional| that day / EOD value; (3) average position size = mean holding weight; (4) cash allocation = cash / total value.
research_questions.prediction: In drawdown, models become more conservative: cash up, HHI down, average position size down; turnover rises transiently as positions are cut.
research_questions.decision_rule: Per model with >=1 drawdown episode and a normal-day contrast: Resolved per metric whose BCa 90% CI on the drawdown-minus-normal difference excludes 0 (live data). Pooled HHI-change test enters the FDR family. If no model draws down in the live window, RQ5 is reported Open / not testable on this tape and deferred to the backtest harness.
research_questions.data_window_required: >=1 episode of >=10% drawdown plus >=20 contrasting normal days per model, live data.

research_questions.title: Non-determinism at temperature = 0

hypothesis.null: At temperature=0, identical inputs yield identical decisions (flip rate = 0).
hypothesis.alternative: Decisions vary across reruns at temperature=0 (flip rate > 0); irreducible API non-determinism.

research_questions.operationalization: Manual-trigger only. On a seeded random 5% subsample of decision ticks, each model is re-run K=10 times at temperature=0 on the same inputs (scripts/determinism_probe.py -> data/determinism/). For each (model, tick, ticker) compare the action across the K reruns.
research_questions.metric_definition: Per model: decision flip rate = fraction of (tick,ticker) decisions where the K reruns do NOT all agree on the action (headline); mean normalized action entropy; mean target-weight standard deviation across reruns.
research_questions.prediction: Flip rate small but non-zero (~1-10%), larger for reasoning models. Sets the empirical ceiling on RQ1 convergence (two models cannot agree more reliably than one model agrees with itself).
research_questions.decision_rule: Descriptive, NOT part of the FDR family. Resolved once K=10 reruns exist for >=5% of a representative live-week's ticks; report per-model flip rate with a binomial 90% interval and use it to bound RQ1.
research_questions.data_window_required: >=1 representative trading week of ticks, 5% sampled, K=10 reruns each, per model.

regime_stratification.description: Every inferential estimate is reported overall and stratified by SPY market regime. Regimes assigned post hoc from SPY daily prices. Volatility annualized as daily-std * sqrt(252).

criteria.range_bound: SPY 20-day return in [-2%, +2%] AND 20-day annualized vol in [10%, 20%]

regime_stratification.note: A day may match multiple rules; precedence yields mutually-exclusive strata. Raw per-rule booleans retained for re-stratification.

multiple_comparison_control.note: One headline frequentist test per RQ forms the family. Per-model sub-tests are exploratory and not folded into the primary family.

sharpe_reporting.method: Deflated Sharpe Ratio (Bailey & Lopez de Prado, 2014) reported alongside raw Sharpe; probabilistic Sharpe available.
sharpe_reporting.n_trials_note: 6 models + SPY = 7 strategies selected from; deflation discounts for skew, kurtosis, sample length, and trials.

bootstrap.method: circular moving-block bootstrap with BCa interval
bootstrap.rationale: Blocks preserve short-horizon autocorrelation; BCa corrects skew/median bias an IID percentile interval ignores.

inference_window.rule: Inferential (confirmatory) analysis uses live-phase data only.
inference_window.paper_phase_role: pilot: pipeline validation, definition-locking, power estimation
inference_window.note: No paper-phase result is reported as confirmatory.

api_nondeterminism.rule: Frontier model APIs are not bitwise deterministic even at temperature=0 (RQ6). Measured RQ1 convergence has a ceiling below 1.0.
api_nondeterminism.interpretation: RQ1 convergence is read relative to (1 - within-model flip rate), never against a naive 100%.

locked_definitions.closed_trade: Closed only on full exit (residual shares < 0.01). Partial trims never close a position; re-entry after exit is an independent trade.
locked_definitions.portfolio_drawdown: EOD portfolio value >= 10% below trailing 60-day peak. Distinct from the SPY drawdown regime (5% off a 60-day peak).
locked_definitions.confidence_to_probability: Predicted win probability p_hat = stated confidence / 10.
locked_definitions.decision_unit: A model's raw_decisions entry per (tick, ticker): {action in BUY/SELL/HOLD, target_weight in [0,1], confidence in 1-10}.
locked_definitions.identical_information_set: Decision records sharing one data_inputs_hash (verified identical across all models within a tick).

data_provenance.immutability: Append-only JSONL committed to git on every tick; full record + inputs hash timestamped; raw model responses retained.
data_provenance.freeze: OSF deposit freezes the v1 design hash.

status_legend.Open: Not enough data yet to test.

amendments.change: Reconciled phase dates to one canonical timeline: paper 2026-04-09 to 2026-10-31; live 2026-11-01 to 2027-11-01 (12-month live window).
amendments.rationale: Aligned settings.json, README.md, PRE_REGISTRATION.md, and this file. Prior draft ended paper 2026-10-09 with an approximate live window. No change to research questions, metrics, or decision rules.
